output: Implemented the approved plan and captured execution evidence. T1-T3 remain completed, T4 was executed and is blocked because two configured Vitest suites still fail. A full recovery checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/investigate-why-locking-the-20260325-1840/execution_checkpoint.json`.
deviations: T4 required rerunning the three requested Vitest suites with `--config config/testing/vitest.config.ts` because the bare `npx vitest run ...` commands did not load the repo's `@/` alias configuration and failed before test discovery.

The overall plan did not finish green: `src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.test.tsx` still fails 1 assertion and `src/shared/contexts/__tests__/PanesContext.test.tsx` still fails 3 assertions under the repo-configured Vitest runner.

task_updates.executor_notes: Removed the `isVideoEditorShellActive ? '0px'` paddingBottom override so the video editor shell now uses the existing `effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight` path for bottom compression. Kept the marginLeft, marginRight, and paddingTop video-editor overrides unchanged, and added `transition-[padding] duration-300 ease-smooth` to the video editor shell container.

Changed the `VideoEditorPage` root container from `h-screen w-screen` to `h-full w-full` so it respects the parent shell's padding-based bottom offset instead of measuring against the viewport. Verified by inspection that the parent video-editor shell main remains `h-full w-full overflow-hidden`, so fill behavior is preserved when no bottom pane offset is applied.

Added two regression tests for the video editor shell path in `LayoutMainContent.test.tsx`: one asserts `paddingBottom: '180px'` when `isVideoEditorShellActive` and `isGenerationsPaneLocked` are true, and one asserts `paddingBottom: '0px'` when the generations pane is neither locked nor open. Verified by inspection that both tests reuse `defaultPanesState`, set `useVideoEditorRouteStateMock.mockReturnValue({ isEditorRoute: true, isVideoEditorShellActive: true })`, and read styles from `container.querySelector('.content-container')`.

Executed the three requested Vitest suites. The initial bare `npx vitest run ...` invocations failed before discovery because the repo aliases were not loaded, so the suites were rerun with the repo-configured `config/testing/vitest.config.ts`. Final verification result: `src/features/layout/hooks/useBottomOffset.test.ts` passed (4/4), but `src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.test.tsx` failed 1 assertion in `renders global layout pieces and applies pane offsets`, and `src/shared/contexts/__tests__/PanesContext.test.tsx` failed 3 assertions around `effectiveEditorHeight` expectations. The batch therefore did not reach an all-green state.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. After the change, `paddingBottom` is `isMobileSplitView ? '0px' : ((isGenerationsPaneLocked || isGenerationsPaneOpen) ? `${effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight}px` : '0px')`, so it still resolves to `'0px'` when the bottom pane is neither locked nor open.

Yes. With the root changed to `h-full w-full`, the page now sizes to the parent `main` container, which is already `h-full w-full overflow-hidden` in video editor mode. When no generations pane padding is applied, the editor still fills the available space correctly.

Yes. Both new tests use the existing `useVideoEditorRouteStateMock.mockReturnValue({ isEditorRoute: true, isVideoEditorShellActive: true })` pattern and query the wrapper with `container.querySelector('.content-container')` before asserting `paddingBottom`.

No. Under the repo's configured Vitest runner, `src/features/layout/hooks/useBottomOffset.test.ts` passed, but `src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.test.tsx` failed 1 test and `src/shared/contexts/__tests__/PanesContext.test.tsx` failed 3 tests, so all three suites did not pass with zero failures.
